author: Austin Crawford
title: Farmers Market Annual Members Meeting
summary: The Farmers Market Annual Members Meeting will be on Sunday, May 29 at 2 p.m. at the picnic tables at Ball O’Brien Park. For those wishing to join the market, the meeting for potential new members will follow at 3 p.m. Please bring samples of what you are submitting to sell; must be handmade, grown or baked on Block Island. Priority is given to produce and other food and drink items.
content: The Farmers Market Annual Members Meeting will be on Sunday, May 29 at 2 p.m. at the picnic tables at Ball O’Brien Park. For those wishing to join the market, the meeting for potential new members will follow at 3 p.m. Please bring samples of what you are submitting to sell; must be handmade, grown or baked on Block Island. Priority is given to produce and other food and drink items.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Zoom on Nature with Coffee and Kim Resumes
summary: The Nature Conservancy’s naturalist on Block Island, is continuing her popular virtual walk series this winter focusing on the island’s unique ecosystems, flora and fauna. December 14 will focus on the many island winter berries. Go to natureblockisland.org to access Zoom.
content: The Nature Conservancy’s naturalist on Block Island, is continuing her popular virtual walk series this winter focusing on the island’s unique ecosystems, flora and fauna. December 14 will focus on the many island winter berries. Go to natureblockisland.org to access Zoom.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Art and Research Residency Talk at Island Free Library
summary: Please join us for a special public program on Saturday, January 10th, from 1:00 to 3:00 PM, featuring talks by Artist Jeff Ostergren and Professor of Sociology at Yale University, Rene Almeling. Jeff and Rene are our first residents attending the Low Season Livesey Residency on Block Island.
content:
Please join us for a special public program on Saturday, January 10th, from 1:00 to 3:00 PM, featuring talks by Artist Jeff Ostergren and Professor of Sociology at Yale University, Rene Almeling. Jeff and Rene are our first residents attending the Low Season Livesey Residency on Block Island.

Hosted by Low Season Artist Projects and sponsored by the Block Island Arts Council, this interdisciplinary event brings together contemporary art, science, and social theory to explore the intersections of chemistry, culture, and climate justice.

title: Jamaica Benefit Concert at Yellow Kittens
summary: Join us for a family-friendly celebration to support our friends in Jamaica! Come enjoy island beats, dancing, tasty food, and good vibes all for a great cause! Suggested Donation: $10
content:

Matt Torrey Trio

Carrie Johnson

Dave Flamand

Hennessey Acoustic Duo

Shawn David Allen

author: Austin Crawford
title: Harbor Church Fall Fair!
summary: Harbor Church Fall Fair! For kids & families!! Ages toddler through 6th grade. There will be music, arts & crafts, jewelry making, games, face painting, prizes, kids tattoos, hot apple cider, and snacks.
content:
Harbor Church Fall Fair! For kids & families!! Ages toddler through 6th grade. There will be music, arts & crafts, jewelry making, games, face painting, prizes, kids tattoos, hot apple cider, and snacks.

 

rain or shine event! outdoor games weather permitting! (plenty of indoor games)

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tango Invasion at Harbor Church
summary: On Sunday, September 14 from 1:00 to 3:30PM the Harbor Church Social Room will be filled with gorgeous Argentine Tango music and Argentine tango dancers  from Providence and Narragansett.
content:
On Sunday, September 14 from 1:00 to 3:30PM the Harbor Church Social Room will be filled with gorgeous Argentine Tango music and Argentine tango dancers  from Providence and Narragansett.
                   EVERYONE IS WELCOME TO JOIN THE DANCING!
Will Feuer will teach a short/simple tango lesson starting at 1:00. This lesson will be interesting for new and experienced tango dancers.
Everyone is welcome with or without a partner.

Questions: Susan Matheke smatheke73@gmail.com

author: Austin Crawford
title: ConserFest Is back
summary: Originating on Block Island, RI, ConserFest is a 501(c)3 organization focused on raising funds and awareness for environmental conservation and stewardship.  We are a strictly grassroots effort relying heavily on volunteers for our events and year-round staff, making us a driving force in raising awareness about local environmental issues. The proceeds and donations from all ConserFest events help to support and fund local efforts dedicated to protecting Block Island's natural environment and raising awareness for the conservation and preservation of local land, heritage and culture.
content:
Originating on Block Island, RI, ConserFest is a 501(c)3 organization focused on raising funds and awareness for environmental conservation and stewardship.  We are a strictly grassroots effort relying heavily on volunteers for our events and year-round staff, making us a driving force in raising awareness about local environmental issues. The proceeds and donations from all ConserFest events help to support and fund local efforts dedicated to protecting Block Island's natural environment and raising awareness for the conservation and preservation of local land, heritage and culture.

The event this year will be held at the 1661 farm and gardens. 

To learn more about the event visit https://m.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=131&v=3rf1bPjpeHA&embeds_euri=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.conserfest.org%2F&feature=emb_logo

https://www.conserfest.org/
address: Spring St New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Shad Bloom 10K
summary: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Dawn. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.
content: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Dawn. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Those Guys at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to groove the night away with Those Guys at Ballard’s Beach Resort this May! Back by popular demand, this energetic party dance band is a favorite among Ballard’s guests, and they’re returning to rock the stage once again. With their infectious beats, soulful melodies, and irresistible charm, Those Guys are sure to keep the party going all night long. Join us for an unforgettable evening filled with great music, dancing, and endless fun!

title: Kenny Chesney Tribute: No Shoes Nation at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to kick off your shoes and join the party with No Shoes Nation at Ballard’s Beach Resort! As a tribute band dedicated to the chart-topping country music star Kenny Chesney, No Shoes Nation brings the spirit of his music to life with passion and energy.

author: Austin Crawford
title: What Matters at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to hit the dance floor with What Matters Band at Ballard’s Beach Resort! Back by popular demand, this high-energy group knows how to keep the party going all night long. With their infectious grooves and irresistible beats, What Matters Band is guaranteed to get everyone up and moving. Whether you’re a fan of classic hits or the latest chart-toppers, their diverse repertoire has something for everyone. Don’t miss your chance to experience the ultimate dance party with What Matters Band at Ballard’s – it’s going to be a summer to remember!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Pulse at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to feel the pulse of the party with Pulse at Ballard’s Beach Resort! This high-energy band brings the fun to the dance floor with their infectious beats and electrifying performances. Whether you’re grooving to the latest hits or jamming out to classic favorites, Pulse guarantees a day filled with non-stop entertainment and unforgettable memories. Don’t miss out on the excitement – join us for an epic summer with Pulse at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Pop Rocks at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to rewind to the raddest decade of all with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s Beach Resort! As the ultimate 80’s pop and rock experience, Pop Rocks takes you on a nostalgic journey through the hits of the iconic era. From chart-topping pop anthems to rock classics, their high-energy performances and infectious stage presence guarantee a night of non-stop fun and dancing. So dust off your leg warmers, tease up your hair, and get ready to rock out to all your favorite 80’s tunes with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: InsideOut at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to dance the night away with InsideOut Band at Ballard’s Beach Resort!  InsideOut is a dynamic Top 40 Dance Cover Band that has been lighting up stages across New England since 2010. With their infectious energy and killer playlist, they’re guaranteed to keep the party going all day long. Join us for a day of non-stop fun and unforgettable moments as InsideOut delivers the ultimate live music experience at Ballard’s!

This high-energy band brings the fun to the dance floor with their infectious beats and electrifying performances. Whether you’re grooving to the latest hits or jamming out to classic favorites, Pulse guarantees a day filled with non-stop entertainment and unforgettable memories. Don’t miss out on the excitement – join us for an epic summer with Pulse at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Brennan McGuire at Ballard's
summary: ALL THE WAY FROM NASHVILLE! Indulge in the vibrant melodies of Brennan McGuire as he graces the stage at Ballard’s Beach Resort. Hailing from Nashville, Brennan brings the essence of country music to Block Island’s shores, infusing each performance with heartfelt lyrics and soulful tunes. Join us for an unforgettable experience as Brennan’s captivating vocals and acoustic rhythms set the perfect ambiance for your beachside retreat. Dive into the sounds of Nashville right here at Ballard’s and let the music transport you to a realm of relaxation and enjoyment.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Shawn David Allen at Ballard's
summary: He was invited to play Summers on the beach in Block Island. One Summer turned into 12 Summer seasons of beachside shows five days each week where he again has entertained thousands upon thousands of vacationers and beach goers.
content:
He was invited to play Summers on the beach in Block Island. One Summer turned into 12 Summer seasons of beachside shows five days each week where he again has entertained thousands upon thousands of vacationers and beach goers.

His full length album, Balancing Act, was produced by longtime friend and Zac Brown Band Bassist -- John Driskell Hopkins. Shawn continues to write his own songs and covers classic rock and blues ranging from Stevie Ray Vaughan to Tom Petty. His playing has brought him to gigs all across the country as well as overseas. Shawn is a professional musician who absolutely loves what he does.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Pop Rocks at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to rewind to the raddest decade of all with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s Beach Resort! As the ultimate 80’s pop and rock experience, Pop Rocks takes you on a nostalgic journey through the hits of the iconic era. From chart-topping pop anthems to rock classics, their high-energy performances and infectious stage presence guarantee a night of non-stop fun and dancing. So dust off your leg warmers, tease up your hair, and get ready to rock out to all your favorite 80’s tunes with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s!
content: Get ready to rewind to the raddest decade of all with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s Beach Resort! As the ultimate 80’s pop and rock experience, Pop Rocks takes you on a nostalgic journey through the hits of the iconic era. From chart-topping pop anthems to rock classics, their high-energy performances and infectious stage presence guarantee a night of non-stop fun and dancing. So dust off your leg warmers, tease up your hair, and get ready to rock out to all your favorite 80’s tunes with Pop Rocks at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Shad Bloom 10K
summary: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Dawn. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.

Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. Questions ? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Dawn. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.

author: BI Chamber
title: Kids and Family Stitch Together
summary: Stop by after school for an introductory class on learning to knit, crochet or sew! All ages welcome – bring what you want to work on, or get some new ideas!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Andy’s Way Bird Walk
summary: A bird walk for all skill levels. Bring binoculars and wear shoes that can get wet. Time changes weekly depending on the tide.
content:
A bird walk for all skill levels. Bring binoculars and wear shoes that can get wet. Time changes weekly depending on the tide.

Meet in Andy's Way Parking Area
Advance sign-up recommended but not required, Email kim.gaffett@tnc.org
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

If the ferry is cancelled race day, then the race will be postponed until Sunday May 15th.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Harvest Festival and Chili Cook-off
summary: Hosted by The Block Island School Friends at the school on Sunday, Oct. 16 from 1 to 4 p.m. Includes a chili cook-off to support the Ceramics Department, games and activities for kids, a bake sale and the Patriots’ game! If you are interested in competing in the chili cook-off or have questions, please call Lisa at (401) 575-3167.
content: Hosted by The Block Island School Friends at the school on Sunday, Oct. 16 from 1 to 4 p.m. Includes a chili cook-off to support the Ceramics Department, games and activities for kids, a bake sale and the Patriots’ game! If you are interested in competing in the chili cook-off or have questions, please call Lisa at (401) 575-3167.
